defects.where: math.0 line 'f(x)=x on [0,1]: each output comes from one input' (~y=318) and math.1 line 'g(x)=x^2: 1/2 and -1/2 both map to 1/4' (~y=378), both horizontally centered on screen

Per the typography note, sentence-level teaching prose should read left-aligned and only short title cards/eyebrows/captions may be centered. Centering teaching clauses creates a slightly ragged, slide-like read rather than an anchored left edge.

audit.verdict_line: VERDICT: 0 visual blocking — this frame has ZERO visual blocking. V1 (no clipping; title, steps, verdicts, takeaway all inside safe margins), V2 (no overlap; clean two-column split with divider rule), V3 (text-slide template, no plot window to misread — N/A and clean), V4 (all Tex renders intact: title f(x)=x / g(x)=x², step math g(½)=¼=g(-½), 'f is one-to-one', 'g is not one-to-one' — no garble, no raw $/backslash, legible), V5 (✓ green on the one-to-one verdict and ✗ red on the not-one-to-one verdict are semantically correct per mark: ok / mark: bad), V6 (frame matches the beat: both functions, both verdicts, the g(½)=¼=g(-½) collision, and the takeaway all present and consistent with the locked say), V7 (fullest frame — all progressive reveals on screen is expected here, not a defect), V8 (visible math is correct vs source: g(1/2)=1/4=g(-1/2) holds, x² exponent and intervals [0,1]/[-1,1] correct). v_findings.evidence: Right panel parabola is plotted as 1.15*x**2 (per payload right.plots[0].expression) but labeled '$y=x^2$'. The two filled crossing dots sit at x=+/-1.043 where the dashed line y=1.25 meets the DRAWN curve 1.15*x^2 (sqrt(1.25/1.15)=1.0425), so they are correctly placed for the curve that is actually rendered.
v_findings.why: Deliberate schematic scaling to fill the panel; label-math is correct and the qualitative two-crossing behavior is what the beat teaches. Per rubric this is a deliberate schematic proportion (label correct, shape approximate) -> at most Advisory, never a blocker. Crossing positions are geometrically consistent with the drawn curve, so no information is lost or contradicted.
v_findings.fix: No change needed for blocking. If exact fidelity to '$y=x^2$' is desired, set expression to 'x**2' and re-derive the dashed-line height / crossing points (e.g. line at y=1.3 -> x=+/-1.14); otherwise leave as a declared schematic.

scene.title: An Inverse Exists Exactly When One-to-One

v_findings.evidence: All on-screen Tex renders cleanly: statement 'A function has an inverse if and only if it is one-to-one.'; proof.0 'If f has an inverse and f(x1)=f(x2), apply f^{-1}: x1=x2. So f is one-to-one.'; proof.1 'If f is one-to-one, each output y has exactly one source x — send y back to it and that is f^{-1}.'; qed 'So one-to-one and invertible name the same thing.' Subscripts x_1/x_2 and superscripts f^{-1} are present and legible; no raw $, no stray backslashes, no missing-glyph boxes, no overflow.
v_findings.why: Math rendering is complete and readable at this size; no information lost. Noted only to record the dimension was actively checked and is clean (not a defect).

defects.where: Lower-right quadrant of the frame (below proof.1 bullet, right of the qed line) is largely empty, while the upper block (statement block + two proof bullets) sits comparatively tight; vertical rhythm is slightly top-heavy.
defects.issue: Element layout is a little top-weighted — content clusters in the upper two-thirds, leaving a noticeable dead zone bottom-right beyond the faint decorative glyph.
defects.suggestion: Increase vertical spacing between the accent statement block and the PROOF eyebrow / bullets so the proof breathes lower into the frame, balancing the empty lower-right; purely an aesthetic rebalance, not information-affecting.

defects.where: The qed line 'So one-to-one and invertible name the same thing.' is set in the accent (teal) color with the QED tombstone box, drawing the eye to the conclusion; the statement block uses a left accent bar but the body stays default text color.
defects.issue: Focus is well-handled overall, but the central theorem statement (the load-bearing 'iff' claim) and the qed line compete slightly for the accent emphasis; the statement could read marginally more prominent than the two-direction proof scaffold.
defects.suggestion: Optional: lightly de-emphasize the proof bullets (or nudge the statement's weight up) so the eye lands first on the iff statement, then the proof, then the qed close. Low priority — current hierarchy already reads correctly.

defects.where: Whole frame — a theorem_proof text template on dark flat background.
defects.issue: Inherently text-forward slide (theorem + word proof has no graph), so it leans toward a flat lecture-slide look more than the graph scenes in this deck.
defects.suggestion: Acceptable for a theorem_proof beat; if extra polish is wanted, the two proof directions could be visually paired (forward/backward) with a subtle two-column or arrow motif. Not a defect of this template choice.

audit.verdict_line: VERDICT: 0 visual blocking — frame 08_inverse_iff_one_to_one matches its locked say/payload (statement, proof.0, proof.1, qed all present and correct as the fullest frame), all Tex renders cleanly (V4), the QED tombstone carries correct end-of-proof semantics (V5), frame agrees with the beat (V6/V7) and the visible math is correct vs source (V8). defects.where: 'y = x' label in the upper-right, on the dashed reflection line (storyboard plot.0, label_point [2.0, 2.3], color_role: muted)
defects.issue: The 'y = x' label renders in a warm red/pink tint, but plot.0 declares color_role: muted (grey, like the dashed line it labels). The label color drifts from the line's declared role, breaking the accent/role consistency with the actual y=x guide line (which is correctly grey).
defects.suggestion: Make the y=x label inherit the muted role of its line so label and dashed line read as one element; reserve the red/accent tint for elements the beat actually wants to highlight.

defects.where: 'y = x' label overlapping the dashed line, upper-right (~[2.0, 2.3])
defects.issue: The dashed line crosses through the label glyphs, mildly reducing crispness of an otherwise comfortable layout.
defects.suggestion: Offset the label clear of the line (small perpendicular nudge) or add a subtle background mask behind it.

defects.where: 'f(x)=x^2' label at [0.55, 2.25] vs the blue parabola's actual position at that height (near x~1.5)
defects.issue: The f(x)=x^2 label sits noticeably to the left of where the blue curve passes at its y-height, so the eye must travel to associate label with curve. It is unambiguous (only the blue curve occupies that upper-left region), so this is minor focus polish, not a collision.
defects.suggestion: Tuck the f(x)=x^2 label nearer the blue curve's upper segment (e.g. shift right toward the steep part) so each label hugs its own curve and the f / f^{-1} pairing reads at a glance.

v_findings.evidence: Right-hand math column (y=f(x) at ~x=1130, x=… at ~x=1135, y=f^{-1}(x) at ~x=1130) sits far right with a wide empty gutter (~x=760 to ~x=1060) between the left prose and the math; no element overlaps or is occluded.
v_findings.why: Nothing is covered or lost — this is a balance/whitespace observation, not occlusion, so it stays Advisory per the escalation rule (merely a little loose, not information-losing).
v_findings.fix: Optionally pull the right-side math column inward (e.g. start math near x=900) so the prose and its math read as one tighter row rather than two far-apart columns.

v_findings.evidence: Worked strip uses two horizontal '→' arrows: y=x^2 → x=±√y → 'not a function' (boxed strip, y≈672). Arrows are sequence/flow connectors, not value-bearing markers.
v_findings.why: Arrows carry the correct flow meaning (each step leads to the next) and match the say beat; no marker is reversed or mis-typed, so this is a clean read, noted only for completeness.
v_findings.fix: No change required; arrows are semantically correct.

audit.verdict_line: VERDICT: 0 visual blocking — frame is clean across V1–V8.

defects.where: Right-side math column (y=f(x), x=⋯, y=f^{-1}(x)) at far right ~x=1060–1200 vs left prose ending ~x=760.
defects.issue: Large horizontal gutter between the step prose and its companion math makes each row read as two disconnected columns rather than one phrase; balance leans empty in the middle band.
defects.suggestion: Reduce the prose-to-math gap (shift the math column left toward ~x=900) so the prose and its restated math feel coupled per step.

defects.where: WORKED strip box (~x=340–1100, y≈630–715) versus the step-number column left margin at ~x=165.
defects.issue: The worked-example box is inset to the right and centered under the step block instead of aligning to the same left margin as the numbered steps, creating a minor alignment drift in the vertical grid.
defects.suggestion: Left-align the worked box (or its 'WORKED' label) to the same x-margin as the step numbers / title for a cleaner shared grid.

defects.where: Right-side restated math y=f(x) / x=⋯ / y=f^{-1(x)} (secondary/cyan) vs the larger accent step numbers 1–3.
defects.issue: The right-column math and the left prose carry similar visual weight; with no single accented focus, the eye is not strongly pulled to the 'current' step (acceptable on a fullest frame, but hierarchy is flatter than ideal).
defects.suggestion: On a fullest frame this is expected; if re-rendering, consider a subtle accent on step 3 / the f^{-1}(x) result to anchor the procedure's payoff.

audit.verdict_line: VERDICT: 0 visual blocking — frame "inverse_of_a_cubic" passes gate 1; all V1-V8 clean, remaining issues are Advisory A-side only.

defects.where: The five-line equation block, center-right of frame; e.g. '=' in line 1 'y = x^3 + 2' (x~895/1920) vs '=' in line 4 'f^{-1}(x) = cbrt(x-2)' (x~840/1920)
defects.issue: [investigated → FALSE POSITIVE] audit read the deliberately-centered chain block as '=' misaligned; geometric measurement (= left-x equal across all lines) + a zoomed crop confirm the '=' DO form a vertical column. No change.
defects.suggestion: none — align_on works as designed.

defects.where: Whole layout: derivation block starts at x~630/1920 and clusters in the right-center/lower-right; the entire upper-left quadrant (below the title, left of x~600) is empty
defects.issue: The equation stack is pushed right and down, leaving a large unbalanced void in the upper-left while content crowds toward center-right. The frame feels off-center rather than using a comfortable visual grid.
defects.suggestion: Left-shift and vertically center the derivation block (or left-align it under the title's left edge) so the negative space is balanced and the equations sit on a consistent left margin shared with the title/eyebrow.

defects.where: Title row, top-left: 'Inverse of' (bold serif) + 'f(x)=x^3+2' (math italic, larger)
defects.issue: The two halves of the title differ in font (serif text vs math), size, and weight, and their baselines/optical sizes do not quite agree, so the title reads as two pasted pieces rather than one typographic unit.
defects.suggestion: Match the math title's cap-height/baseline to the serif lead-in (scale the math down slightly and baseline-align) so 'Inverse of f(x)=x^3+2' reads as a single title line.

defects.where: Line 4 (gold) 'f^{-1}(x) = cbrt(x-2)' vs the surrounding light-blue lines
defects.issue: The gold highlight on the result line works, but the four scaffold lines are all the same brightness/size, so the eye is not strongly guided through the build order before landing on the result; the scaffold does not visibly recede.
defects.suggestion: Slightly dim/desaturate the already-spoken scaffold lines (or reduce their weight) so the highlighted result f^{-1}(x)=cbrt(x-2) reads as the focal point.

v_findings.evidence: Title inline formula 'f(x)=3/(x-5)' is rendered oversized; its numerator '3' reaches near the top safe area (~y150 of 1080) and the bottom derivation line 'f^{-1}(x)=5+3/x=(5x+3)/x' sits ~y880. Everything stays inside the frame; nothing is clipped.
v_findings.why: No key element (title formula, any of the 4 derivation lines, the highlighted answer) is cropped or pushed past the frame edge — no information is lost, so this stays Advisory rather than Blocking.
v_findings.fix: No action required for correctness; the only adjustment worth making is aesthetic (shrink the oversized title formula, see A6 defect).

v_findings.evidence: All Tex renders cleanly: title 'Inverse of f(x)=3/(x-5)', and lines 'y=3/(x-5)', 'x-5=3/y', 'x=5+3/y', 'f^{-1}(x)=5+3/x=(5x+3)/x'. Fraction vincula, the superscript -1, and all digits are intact — no raw '$', no stray backslash, no missing/overflowing glyphs.
v_findings.why: Every glyph carrying teaching value is fully formed and large enough to read; reporting this only to confirm the dimension is clean (not a defect).

v_findings.evidence: Frame shows the title formula plus exactly the four payload `lines` (y=3/(x-5); x-5=3/y; x=5+3/y; f^{-1}(x)=5+3/x=(5x+3)/x). The `say` also speaks a both-direction check and a domain-pairing remark, but those are intentionally verbal-only (not in the `lines` payload), so their absence from the frame is expected, not a contradiction.
v_findings.why: Nothing on screen conflicts with what this beat teaches; the visible content is a faithful subset of the locked payload.

v_findings.evidence: Visible algebra checks out: y=3/(x-5) -> x-5=3/y -> x=5+3/y -> f^{-1}(x)=5+3/x, and 5+3/x = (5x+3)/x is algebraically identical. Matches the storyboard `lines` verbatim.
v_findings.why: All visible math is correct against the source; no value, sign, or form is wrong.

defects.where: Title row, top: the word 'Inverse of' (serif, baseline ~y320) followed by the inline formula 'f(x)=3/(x-5)' which is set noticeably larger and floats UP relative to the word — its center sits ~y250 and numerator ~y150, so the two pieces of the same title do not share a baseline or a type size.
defects.issue: Sentence/title-card text and its inline math are baseline-misaligned and size-mismatched, making the title card read as two disjoint pieces rather than one phrase. (A6 wants math and adjacent text baseline-aligned.)
defects.suggestion: Render the title formula at a size matched to 'Inverse of' and vertically center it on the same baseline (or move the whole formula onto a single baseline with the word), so 'Inverse of f(x)=3/(x-5)' reads as one line.

defects.where: Overall composition: the 4 derivation lines are clustered in a right-of-center column (~x800-1230), leaving the entire left half empty, plus a large vertical gap between the title block (top ~third) and the first derivation line (mid-frame).
defects.issue: Layout is unbalanced — content hugs the right while the left and the upper-middle are dead space, so the frame does not use a comfortable, evenly-distributed visual grid.
defects.suggestion: Center the derivation column (or left-align it under the title) and tighten the title->first-line gap, distributing the four lines to fill the vertical space more evenly.

defects.where: Focus competition: the oversized white title formula (top) is visually as loud as the amber-accent final answer line 'f^{-1}(x)=5+3/x=(5x+3)/x' (bottom), the element this derivation is meant to land on.
defects.issue: The intended focal point (the highlighted result) competes for attention with an over-large title formula; the eye is not cleanly steered to the answer.
defects.suggestion: Reduce the title formula's size (per A6 fix) so the accent-colored answer line is unambiguously the strongest element on the frame.

defects.where: Type-size consistency between the title inline formula (top, oversized) and the body derivation math (cyan/amber lines), which are set at a smaller, consistent size among themselves.
defects.issue: The title math is set at a different (larger) scale than the body math, a mild inconsistency in the math type scale across the frame.
defects.suggestion: Bring the title formula into the same relative scale family as the body math so the math typography is consistent.

audit.verdict_line: VERDICT: 0 visual blocking — this final frame of scene 'inverse_of_a_rational' has zero Blocking findings (V1-V8 all clean: nothing cropped, nothing overlapping/covering info, math fully rendered and legible, all visible algebra correct vs the storyboard `lines`, consistent with the beat, all elements present as expected on the fullest frame). It converges the visual blocking gate. Remaining issues are Advisory/aesthetic only — an oversized, baseline-misaligned title formula and a right-clustered/unbalanced layout — captured in A6/A1/A7/A4 defects to prioritize a re-render polish, not to block.

scene.title: An Inverse That Answers a Question

v_findings.evidence: Math block, line 2 (counting from top): 't = 9/5 f^{-1}(t) + 32'. This is a non-standard intermediate step — it substitutes f^{-1}(t) into the forward formula's input slot and sets the result equal to t. It renders cleanly and matches storyboard source line.1 verbatim (line 444-446 of ch01_inverse_functions.yml).
v_findings.why: Not a render or math error: the line is mathematically the setup 't = f(f^{-1}(t))' written out, and it is exactly the authored payload, so nothing is lost or contradicted. Flagged only as Advisory awareness because, read in isolation, this intermediate line is slightly unusual pedagogically (it pre-supposes the F->C reading before solving). No information loss -> stays Advisory per escalation rule.
v_findings.fix: No render fix required. If the author wants line 2 to read more transparently as a composition setup, consider rewriting source line.1 to 't = f(f^{-1}(t)) = 9/5 f^{-1}(t) + 32'; but this is an authoring/content choice, not a visual blocker — leave as-is unless the narration audit (NFA) flags it.

defects.where: The four-line derivation block (center of frame). Equals signs across the four lines: line1 'f(t) =', line2 't =', line3 'f^{-1}(t) =', line4 'f^{-1}(f(t)) =' do not vertically align.
defects.issue: [investigated → FALSE POSITIVE] audit read the deliberately-centered chain block as '=' misaligned; geometric measurement (= left-x equal across all lines) + a zoomed crop confirm the '=' DO form a vertical column. No change.
defects.suggestion: none — align_on works as designed.

defects.where: Vertical placement of the math block relative to the title.
defects.issue: The four equations sit low-center, leaving a large empty band between the title/eyebrow (top ~12%) and the first equation (~44% down). The bottom third below the last line is also mostly empty save the logo.
defects.suggestion: Nudge the derivation block up ~5-8% toward optical center for a more balanced top-to-bottom rhythm. Minor; the dark flat background and final-frame fullness are house style and not defects.

defects.where: Line 4 (composition check) vs line 3 (highlighted result).
defects.issue: Line 4 'f^{-1}(f(t)) = ... = t' is rendered in the same blue as the scaffold lines, so the closing check reads as ordinary scaffold rather than as the confirming payoff of the derivation.
defects.suggestion: Optional: give the trailing '= t' a subtle accent or mark to signal the check closes successfully (mirrors mark: ok usage elsewhere in this deck). Low priority — current state is consistent, just not emphatic.

audit.verdict_line: VERDICT: 0 visual blocking. This frame has zero V1-V8 blocking findings — all four equations render cleanly, match the authored payload (storyboard scene id: inverse_in_an_application, lines 444-448), and the visible math is correct (C->F formula, F->C inverse 5/9(t-32), and the composition check 5/9(9/5 t + 32 - 32) = t). The orange highlight correctly marks the key result line. One Advisory (V8) notes a non-standard but author-intended intermediate line; A-side polish is held back mainly by A6 (declared align_on:"=" not honored — block is centered, equals column not aligned). Visual blocking is zero for this section frame; advisories left for author triage.
VERDICT: 0 visual blocking — post-fix regression: point 04 caveat fully inside bottom safe margin; no new defect.

defects.suggestion: optional: nudge cards to mirror left-column vertical extent

defects.issue: three cards differ slightly in height/internal padding
defects.suggestion: optional: normalize card padding
